posts.text: It's Friyay--so let's have some fun. Who is celebrating 🌈 Pride this year?? Last year, I put out a request for any branded Pride swag--preferably stickers, so I can deck out my laptop. Send whatever you have my way, and I'll give you a shoutout... and sport your brand at every event for the next year! 😇 Last year's allies: ClearCo Experian HiBob HiredScore Hireguide HR Bytes™ Community ZooKeep (Global)

posts.text: Today, MeBeBot is launching something we've been working on for a while and something we think the HR and IT community genuinely needs right now. Recognition! Introducing the Humanity in AI Awards. Too many AI deployments optimize for efficiency and forget the humans on the other end. These awards exist to change that narrative. We're recognizing 9 leaders and teams in HR, Operations, and IT who are getting AI right for people, not just getting AI deployed. There are three categories: 🏆 AI Literacy & Enablement For the leader or team that did the most to prepare their workforce for AI, through training, skill-building, change management, or communications that brought employees along rather than imposing AI on them. 🏆 Human-Centric AI Implementation For the leader or team that deployed AI in a way that measurably improved employees' daily work lives, reducing friction, speeding up answers, eliminating busywork, and creating more time for meaningful work. 🏆 AI Trust & Governance For the leader or team that built the most thoughtful framework for responsible AI use, acceptable use policies, data protection, transparency practices, audit mechanisms, or cross-functional governance structures. 3 category winners each receive a $500 gift card. 6 finalists receive dedicated blog and LinkedIn spotlights. All 9 honorees receive a custom digital badge and recognition at our virtual ceremony on August 6. Our judges are Felicia Newhouse, PhD of AI-Powered Women, George LaRocque of WORKTECH, and Zoë Walters of Empowrd AI bring decades of combined experience in HR technology, AI ethics, and organizational transformation. Nominations are open today. They close July 24. You can nominate yourself, someone else, or a team. Multiple nominations are welcome. If you know someone who is getting AI right for people, this is the moment to say so publicly. Nominate at https://lnkd.in/dP9dNuec 👇 #HumanityInAI #AIAwards #AIGovernance #AILiteracy #AIWorkforceTransformation
posts.hook: Today, MeBeBot is launching something we've been working on for a while and something we think the HR and IT community genuinely needs right…
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: You know that feeling when you have so many thoughts on a topic, you can't quite say what you mean? Well I recently joined my ol' pal David Wachtel on Workday's Future of Work podcast to talk about... you guessed it—AI in HR. 😁 Several of the questions he asked had my mind working in overdrive, and there was one point I don't think I quite landed. It's arguably the most salient point from the entire convo, so I took some time to go deeper on it: HR isn't losing ground to AI. HR is ceding it. There's a difference between being a stakeholder in AI transformation and being a steward of it. And right now, most HR functions are firmly in the first camp—getting consulted, weighing in, occasionally pumping the brakes. But not leading. I wrote about why that distinction matters—and what it actually takes to close the gap. You can find it on the blog: https://lnkd.in/eag9PW9n And you can tune into the pod: https://lnkd.in/euQPWFNm
posts.hook: You know that feeling when you have so many thoughts on a topic, you can't quite say what you mean?
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: I was in the room at Cornerstone OnDemand's analyst day and Connect roadshow for the launch of Cornerstone Workforce AI. Kyle & Co was invited to publish an honest, independent read on it. I'm working on my first-ever vlog for a review, but I needed to put my thoughts together first. A few takeaways worth sharing. Workforce AI is the most competitive answer Cornerstone has built in years. Against SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, Eightfold AI, and the broader talent intelligence category. If you're a Cornerstone customer, the platform you bought just got meaningfully more powerful. The real bet, though, is bigger than the launch made it sound. Talent intelligence vendors (e.g. Visier Inc., Lightcast, Revelio, Horsefly, TalentNeuron) built powerful platforms. But they're systems of insight sitting alongside the systems of action where work actually gets done. Insight that isn't actionable is a novelty—and that's leaving talent intelligence a little vulnerable when HR teams are under pressure to optimize their budgets. Cornerstone's move is different—with Workforce AI, they collapse that gap from the systems-of-action side. Put the intelligence layer and the action layer in the same box. That's the real thesis. Underneath all of this is something almost no one has covered: Cornerstone made a category-level pricing bet. Most AI HR vendors are pricing on consumption—per token, per API call, per agent action. Cornerstone walked away from that. Workforce AI is priced in outcome-tied bundles, no token charges, no API fees. That's the boldest commitment to value over consumption any major HR tech vendor has made this year. Which changes everything about what comes next. Because under consumption pricing, customer ignorance is revenue. Under outcome-based pricing, customer ignorance is churn. Cornerstone now has a direct commercial stake in helping customers actually operate what they bought. Full analysis here: https://lnkd.in/dbRNKbEi
posts.hook: I was in the room at Cornerstone OnDemand's analyst day and Connect roadshow for the launch of Cornerstone Workforce AI.
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: The Human-Centric AI Council was built on two powerful ideas: 1. That the people side of AI transformation is the hard part. 2. That HR function needs to be ready to lead it. As you know, we pulled together a group of senior HR and talent leaders to address these ideas--and we have spent the last year building the toolkit to do exactly that. Today, we're sharing it publicly for the first time in our latest HCAIC Town Hall--delivered in partnership with Purple Acorn and with Brandice P. weaving their magic wand. Four resources + the practitioners who built them... And a real conversation about what responsible AI in HR actually looks like in practice rather than in theory. This one's open to everyone. Pull up a chair! Joining me will be several of lead contributors (and a couple of new faces!): Adam Treitler Alicia Miller Katie Burke Fairbank Rachel Bourne Tara Torres You can register to get the recording: https://lnkd.in/eae6P8VK Or you can dial in at 1:00p ET: https://lnkd.in/eyyDyfAQ
posts.hook: The Human-Centric AI Council was built on two powerful ideas: 1.
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: Not all MCP's are created equal. For starters: Some are able to produce way more insight than others (and in a super fun way). I tried Decoded by Findem Studio and the output shows me what data + context can produce. It's really cool. Out of over 200k HR Tech industry voices in Findem's public talent graph, only 3 matched my full signal stack—multi-firm operator track from IDC to Beamery to General Motors and back, principal-analyst voice with founder-level continuity, and Emerson College-trained storytelling. Even more interesting: the system matched my career DNA at 91% with none other than Josh Bersin and tagged my archetype as: "The Analyst Who Crossed Every Side of the Table" Pretty fascinating look at how AI interprets professional identity, influence, and career patterns--especially when it utilizes expertly labeled data. If you want to see your own profile breakdown, comment "Findem Studio" and I'll point you to it. #TalentIntelligence #HRTech #FutureOfWork #AI #IndependentAnalyst H/T to Joseph Cole, Liv Anderman and Frances Wei for this amazing idea!
posts.hook: Not all MCP's are created equal.
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: Leadership has always been hard. I learned that lesson firsthand--and it nearly broke me. The good news is that there's a new generation of leaders in #hrtech and #hrtransformation who are strong enough to bear the uniquely heavy burden that the next chapter of AI in HR will bring. More than that, there are leaders driving innovation that's more than novel: it's useful. In my latest blog, I share a different kind of trend I think will shape what comes next: Not just what's built, but who's building it. TLDR (and it really *is* too long, sorry): The kids will be alright. Three vendors and leaders called out specifically (with two more write-ups on the content calendar): Hariharan Kolam and the Findem/Glider AI teams are raising the bar on AI-enabled talent operations with unique approaches to fraud, data, and assessment. Benjamin Encz and the Ashby team are reigniting the ATS market with products that illicited Oohs, Aahs, and applause at Open. Gerrit Kazmaier and the Workday team are redefining the requirements of HCM's core systems of record--and it's powered by a lot more than their acquisition of Sana. Check it out: https://lnkd.in/e64TDJFe
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas

posts.text: Two years ago, every vendor was racing to ship GenAI use cases. *Some* of it was genuinely useful. A lot of it was feature theater. Buyers got skeptical fast—and rightfully so! Now the market is sorting itself out. The vendors that invested in the right foundations are pulling ahead. The ones that bolted AI onto legacy architecture, fragmented datasets, or isolated workflows are starting to show cracks in their value propositions. Cornerstone OnDemand is pushing hard to be recognized in that former camp. And I'll be honest: What little I know about what they're bringing to market right now can definitely keep them there through this next chapter of AI in HR. The Cornerstone team invited Kyle & Co to #CornerstoneConnect26 in New York next week to capture the moment firsthand—analyst preview on Tuesday, followed by announcement realness on Wednesday. Brandice and I will be talking to customers, leaders, and maybe an analyst or two on the ground about what the launch actually means for their work. Not the keynote version. The real version. I'll be sharing what I see, what I think, and (as always) what HR teams can really expect. Stay tuned. Cornerstone Connect is May 20 in New York. If you want to join, hit the link: https://lnkd.in/esFf8cZu
posts.hook: Two years ago, every vendor was racing to ship GenAI use cases.
posts.name: Kyle Lagunas
